recommendations.title: Address Denial of Service vulnerabilities
recommendations.description: 173 Denial of Service issues detected. Review and apply secure coding best practices.

recommendations.title: Address Insecure Randomness vulnerabilities
recommendations.description: 9 Insecure Randomness issues detected. Review and apply secure coding best practices.

recommendations.title: Address Vulnerable Dependency vulnerabilities
recommendations.description: 7 Vulnerable Dependency issues detected. Review and apply secure coding best practices.

recommendations.title: Address SSRF vulnerabilities
recommendations.description: 7 SSRF issues detected. Review and apply secure coding best practices.

recommendations.title: Address Hardcoded Secret vulnerabilities
recommendations.description: 4 Hardcoded Secret issues detected. Review and apply secure coding best practices.

vulnerabilities.title: JWT token detected in source
vulnerabilities.description: A signed JWT was found hardcoded in the source. Hard-coded tokens bypass authentication if the signature is trusted.
vulnerabilities.fix: Remove the secret from source code. Store it in environment variables or a secrets manager (e.g. HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager). Rotate the credential immediately.

vulnerabilities.title: JWT token detected in source

vulnerabilities.title: JWT token detected in source

vulnerabilities.title: Google Cloud Platform API key detected
vulnerabilities.description: A GCP/Google API key was found. Exposure can lead to unauthorised API usage and billing.
vulnerabilities.fix: Remove the secret from source code. Store it in environment variables or a secrets manager (e.g. HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager). Rotate the credential immediately.

vulnerabilities.title: Potential Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)
vulnerabilities.description: Regex patterns with nested quantifiers or alternations on overlapping character classes can cause catastrophic backtracking when matched against adversarial input.
vulnerabilities.fix: Rewrite the regex to eliminate ambiguous repetitions, or use a linear-time regex engine.

vulnerabilities.title: SSRF via fetch() with concatenated URL
vulnerabilities.description: Making HTTP requests to URLs constructed from user input allows Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF), potentially exposing internal services.
vulnerabilities.fix: Validate URLs against an allowlist of trusted domains and schemes before issuing requests.

vulnerabilities.title: Use of Math.random() for security-sensitive purpose
vulnerabilities.description: Math.random() is not cryptographically secure and must not be used for tokens, keys, or nonces.
vulnerabilities.fix: Use crypto.randomBytes() or crypto.getRandomValues() for all security-sensitive values.

vulnerabilities.title: Prototype pollution via recursive merge / set
vulnerabilities.description: Merging or setting properties from user-controlled objects without key filtering allows attackers to pollute Object.prototype.
vulnerabilities.fix: Use Object.create(null) for merge targets, filter keys against __proto__, constructor, prototype, and use structured clone for deep copies.

vulnerabilities.title: Prototype pollution via recursive merge / set
vulnerabilities.description: Merging or setting properties from user-controlled objects without key filtering allows attackers to pollute Object.prototype.
vulnerabilities.fix: Use Object.create(null) for merge targets, filter keys against __proto__, constructor, prototype, and use structured clone for deep copies.

vulnerabilities.title: Use of Math.random() for security-sensitive purpose
vulnerabilities.description: Math.random() is not cryptographically secure and must not be used for tokens, keys, or nonces.
vulnerabilities.fix: Use crypto.randomBytes() or crypto.getRandomValues() for all security-sensitive values.

vulnerabilities.title: Use of Math.random() for security-sensitive purpose
vulnerabilities.description: Math.random() is not cryptographically secure and must not be used for tokens, keys, or nonces.
vulnerabilities.fix: Use crypto.randomBytes() or crypto.getRandomValues() for all security-sensitive values.

vulnerabilities.title: JSON.parse without schema validation
vulnerabilities.description: Parsing JSON from untrusted sources without subsequent schema validation can introduce unexpected types or prototype-pollution gadgets.
vulnerabilities.fix: Validate the parsed object against a strict schema (e.g., zod, joi) before use.

vulnerabilities.title: Potential Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)
vulnerabilities.description: Regex patterns with nested quantifiers or alternations on overlapping character classes can cause catastrophic backtracking when matched against adversarial input.
vulnerabilities.fix: Rewrite the regex to eliminate ambiguous repetitions, or use a linear-time regex engine.

vulnerabilities.title: SSRF via fetch() with concatenated URL
vulnerabilities.description: Making HTTP requests to URLs constructed from user input allows Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF), potentially exposing internal services.
vulnerabilities.fix: Validate URLs against an allowlist of trusted domains and schemes before issuing requests.

vulnerabilities.title: API endpoint returning unbounded data (missing pagination)
vulnerabilities.description: A route fetches all records from the database without visible pagination, risking DoS via large result sets.
vulnerabilities.fix: Add pagination: db.find({ limit: req.query.limit ?? 50, offset: req.query.offset ?? 0 })
